description: FD6 calibration corpus for the duplicate-build guard's token-set-Jaccard fuzzy floor (docs/specs/duplicate-build-guard.md §3.1/FD6). Each pair is a title+body PAIR in instar's real vocabulary: `duplicates` are known same-feature pairs (including the 2026-07-12 PR #1458 vs #1460 incident pair, reworded/renamed variants); `nonDuplicates` are known DIFFERENT features that deliberately share heavy platform vocabulary (telegram/session/secret/queue/gate) — the adversarial precision cases. The unit test asserts full recall on `duplicates` and a precision floor on `nonDuplicates` at exactly the shipped JACCARD_THRESHOLD. Changing the threshold constant requires re-passing this corpus — an evidence-set constant, not a guess.

a.title: LLM-Decision Quality Meter (ACT-1193/1194)
a.body: Record provenance for every LLM decision point: a census ratchet over decision points so a new LLM decision that skips provenance fails CI. Each enrolled callsite writes a decision_quality row with verdict, model, and outcome so gates can be graded. Adds the provenance coverage census file and wires the first decision points.

b.title: LLM-decision provenance wiring (ACT-562)
b.body: Wire LLM decision points into provenance recording so every decision an LLM gate makes is captured and gradable. Introduces a coverage census over decision points, enrolls callsites with provenance options, and records decision quality outcomes per verdict and model for later grading.

a.title: Duplicate build detector for instar-dev
a.body: Catch a build that is already in flight under a different tracking id BEFORE the build cost is spent. Check open pull requests and recently merged commits for the same substrate files and decision points, plus a sibling session ledger on the same machine.

b.title: Duplicate-Build Guard — overlap check at build start
b.body: Detect that another session or pull request is already building the same feature before a whole session is wasted. Scans open PRs, recent merges to main, and a local in-flight ledger for overlapping substrate files and decision-point targets.

a.title: Telegram delivery retry queue
a.body: A durable retry queue for failed Telegram sends: failed deliveries are persisted, retried with exponential backoff, and survive server restarts so no user message is silently lost.

b.title: Durable retry for failed Telegram message delivery
b.body: Failed Telegram sends go into a durable persisted queue with backoff and are retried after a restart, so a delivery failure never silently drops the user's message.

a.title: Session reaper pressure-aware idle kill
a.body: The idle-session reaper considers CPU load per core and memory pressure when deciding whether to kill idle sessions, raising pressure tiers on a CPU-bound machine even when free memory is fine.

b.title: CPU-aware session reaping under load
b.body: Make the idle session reaper consider per-core CPU load and memory pressure in its kill decisions so a CPU-bound box raises the pressure tier and reaps idle sessions earlier.

a.title: Cross-machine secret sync
a.body: A secret entered on one machine is encrypted to each peer machine's key and pushed to registered paired machines automatically, so a credential dropped once is usable everywhere without re-entering it per machine.

b.title: Sync secrets across the machine pool
b.body: Push encrypted secrets to every registered peer machine so a credential the user drops on one machine works on all of them — encrypted per recipient machine key, never plaintext on disk.

duplicates.name: fully rewritten problem statement, same feature (rename + rewrite)

a.title: Stuck-session watchdog with escalating kill sequence
a.body: Detect sessions whose process is wedged and recover them: watch for stalled output, nudge first, then escalate through progressively stronger kill signals until the session is freed, notifying the user when recovery fails.

b.title: SessionWatchdog escalation ladder
b.body: A watchdog that notices a wedged session process, tries a gentle nudge, and walks an escalating kill sequence when the session stays stuck, with user-facing watchdog notifications on failure.

nonDuplicates.name: telegram retry vs telegram topic flood guard (shared: telegram, queue, budget)

a.title: Telegram delivery retry queue
a.body: A durable retry queue for failed Telegram sends: failed deliveries are persisted, retried with exponential backoff, and survive server restarts so no user message is silently lost.

b.title: Telegram topic flood guard
b.body: A per-source circuit breaker at the topic-creation chokepoint: when one attention source exceeds its topic budget within a rolling window, further non-critical items are coalesced into one running notices topic instead of a wall of new topics.

nonDuplicates.name: session reaper vs session clock (shared: session, time)

a.title: Session reaper pressure-aware idle kill
a.body: The idle-session reaper considers CPU load per core and memory pressure when deciding whether to kill idle sessions, raising pressure tiers on a CPU-bound machine even when free memory is fine.

b.title: Session clock elapsed and remaining API
b.body: A read-only endpoint reporting computed elapsed and remaining time for any active time-boxed session so the agent never estimates elapsed or remaining time by hand.

nonDuplicates.name: secret sync vs secret drop (shared: secret, credential, machine)

a.title: Cross-machine secret sync
a.body: A secret entered on one machine is encrypted to each peer machine's key and pushed to registered paired machines automatically, so a credential dropped once is usable everywhere without re-entering it per machine.

b.title: Secret Drop one-time collection links
b.body: Securely collect credentials from users without exposing them in chat history: a one-time expiring form link with CSRF protection; the submitted value is persisted encrypted and consumed once.

nonDuplicates.name: decision quality meter vs token ledger (shared: LLM, record, observability)

a.title: LLM-Decision Quality Meter
a.body: Record provenance for every LLM decision point: a census ratchet over decision points so a new LLM decision that skips provenance fails CI. Each enrolled callsite writes a decision quality row so gates can be graded.

b.title: Token ledger read-only usage observability
b.body: Scan Claude Code JSONL transcripts for token usage into a SQLite-backed ledger, exposing summary and per-session endpoints. Never gates or mutates source files — observability only.

nonDuplicates.name: duplicate detector vs green-PR auto-merge (shared: PR, merge, CI, build)

a.title: Duplicate build detector for instar-dev
a.body: Catch a build that is already in flight under a different tracking id BEFORE the build cost is spent. Check open pull requests and recently merged commits for the same substrate files and decision points.

b.title: Green PR auto-merge watcher
b.body: When one of the agent's own pull requests goes green, a background watcher arms GitHub native auto-merge so the merge happens the instant every required check passes; holds and labels always win.

nonDuplicates.name: cartographer vs playbook (shared: context, summaries, freshness)

a.title: Cartographer doc-tree freshness sweep
a.body: A background sweep that re-authors stale node summaries in the hierarchical semantic code map on a light model, with a CI ratchet keeping aggregate freshness from backsliding.

b.title: Playbook adaptive context manifest
b.body: A living knowledge base of curated context items — facts, lessons, patterns, safety rules — selected per session by triggers, token budgets, and usefulness scores instead of loading static context every time.

nonDuplicates.name: rope health vs load assessment (shared: machine, health, verdict)

a.title: Mesh rope health alerts
a.body: Classify each peer machine's connectivity per rope kind every 30 seconds — ok, degraded, peer-offline, or urgent partition — raising one deduped attention item per episode with honest latency bounds.

b.title: Machine load assessment script
b.body: Report the machine's real CPU idle percentage, agent-attributed CPU over the last hour, and per-core load, then a verdict of OK, ELEVATED, or SATURATED — because the 1-minute load average is the wrong signal.

nonDuplicates.name: attention breaker vs commitment beacon (shared: queue, cadence, item)

a.title: Attention queue circuit breaker
a.body: Budget topic creation per attention source within a rolling window and coalesce overflow items into one running topic, recording suppressed items durably so nothing is dropped.

b.title: Commitment tracker beacon cadence
b.body: Cadenced heartbeats on open beacon-enabled commitments with an atRisk non-terminal state and boot-cap enforcement, so a promise to the user survives session turnover and actually gets delivered.
